title: Wire Concurrency module features to platform MODULE_FEATURES progress tracking
description:
Platform has MODULE_FEATURES progress tracking at lib/progress/module-progress.ts but Concurrency module features need to be verified and matched to the actual 12 demos. Currently the concurrency features array should list all demo IDs. When a user views a demo and plays through it, the feature should be marked as explored. This connects to the dashboard progress display, 'Continue where you left off,' and the skill radar chart.

Check: does the concurrency entry in MODULE_FEATURES match the actual 12 demos (race-condition, producer-consumer, dining-philosophers, event-loop, thread-lifecycle, go-goroutines, readers-writers, sleeping-barber, async-patterns, deadlock-demo, lock-comparison, thread-pool-saturation)?
acceptanceCriteria: MODULE_FEATURES concurrency entry lists all 12 demo IDs
Viewing a demo marks it as explored in localStorage
Dashboard shows correct concurrency progress percentage
Skill radar chart includes concurrency module data

title: Add Concurrency-specific cross-module bridges beyond the 2 existing system-design bridges
description:
The bridge registry has only 2 concurrency bridges — both pointing to system-design. Missing critical cross-module connections:
1. Concurrency → Database: 'Mutex/Lock → Database Locks (Row lock, Table lock, MVCC)'
2. Concurrency → Database: 'Readers-Writers → Read Replicas / MVCC'
3. Concurrency → Distributed: 'Deadlock → Distributed Deadlock Detection'
4. Concurrency → Distributed: 'CAS → Distributed Consensus (CAS is the local equivalent)'
5. Concurrency → Networking: 'Event Loop → epoll/select/kqueue'
6. Concurrency → OS: 'Thread Lifecycle → Process Scheduling'
7. Concurrency → OS: 'Context Switching → OS Scheduler'
8. Concurrency → Data Structures: 'Lock-Free DS → Concurrent Skip List, CAS-based Stack'
9. Concurrency → LLD: 'Producer-Consumer → Message Queue Pattern'
10. Concurrency → LLD: 'Thread Pool → Object Pool Pattern'

Add to bridge-registry.ts with appropriate payloadFactory functions.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 8 new concurrency bridges added to BRIDGE_REGISTRY
Bridges cover connections to Database, Distributed, Networking, OS, Data Structures, and LLD modules
Each bridge has a descriptive label and description
RecommendedBridges component shows relevant bridges when concurrency module is active

title: Add Concurrency concepts to concept-module-map for cross-module knowledge graph
description:
The concept-module-map.ts (245 lines) maps concepts across modules but may not include all concurrency concepts. Ensure all 12 existing + planned concepts are mapped:
- Mutex → Database locks, OS kernel locks
- Semaphore → Database connection pools, OS resource limits
- Deadlock → Database transaction deadlocks, Distributed deadlock
- Race condition → Database lost updates, Distributed split-brain
- Event loop → Networking I/O models, OS scheduling
- Producer-Consumer → Message queues (Kafka/RabbitMQ)
- Thread pool → Web server architecture (Tomcat, Nginx)
- Goroutines → Go microservice patterns
acceptanceCriteria: All 12 concurrency concepts mapped to related concepts in other modules
ConceptModuleLinks component shows relevant cross-module links in concurrency properties panel
Knowledge Graph module can visualize concurrency concept connections

title: Add Concurrency-specific daily challenges to the dashboard challenge pool
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 concurrency challenges added to DAILY_CHALLENGES array
Challenges span Easy/Medium/Hard difficulty
Clicking a concurrency challenge navigates to the concurrency module with the relevant demo active

title: Verify Concurrency module keyboard shortcuts are consistent with platform-wide shortcuts
description:
The platform has keyboard-shortcuts-dialog.tsx and use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts for global shortcuts. The activity bar shows shortcuts 1-9 for modules (concurrency = '9'). Verify:
1. Pressing '9' switches to concurrency module from any other module
2. Cmd+K opens command palette with concurrency demos searchable
3. Concurrency-specific shortcuts (Play/Step/Reset) don't conflict with global shortcuts
4. All shortcuts listed in keyboard-shortcuts-dialog include concurrency module's controls
acceptanceCriteria: '9' key switches to concurrency module from any module
Command palette search finds all 12 concurrency demos by name
No keyboard shortcut conflicts between concurrency playback and global shortcuts
Keyboard shortcuts dialog lists concurrency-specific controls

title: Add Concurrency module to SEO concept pages and blog data
description:
The platform has SEO infrastructure: concepts/page.tsx, blog/page.tsx, and seo/concepts-data.ts. Verify concurrency concepts are included in SEO-optimized pages:
1. Check if concurrency concepts appear in seo/concepts-data.ts
2. Check if concurrency has entries in seo/blog-data.ts
3. Verify /concepts/[slug] routes exist for concurrency topics
4. Verify Open Graph metadata for concurrency pages
acceptanceCriteria: All 12 concurrency demos have entries in seo/concepts-data.ts
At least 3 concurrency blog post stubs exist in seo/blog-data.ts
Concurrency concept pages are crawlable by search engines
Open Graph images generated for concurrency concept pages
